provenance.source_corpus: Effects of cover soil stockpiling on plant community development following reclamation of oil sands sites in Alberta

source_spans.text: Stockpiling of cover soil can influence vegetation development following reclamation.
Direct placement of cover soil resulted in higher percent cover, species richness, and diversity.

nodes.name: Availability and vigor of vegetative propagules and seed

source_spans.text: stockpiling reduces the availability and vigor of vegetative propagules and seed
can lead to increases in the abundance of some weedy species
Direct placement of cover soil resulted in higher percent cover, species richness, and diversity.
Direct placement of cover soil resulted in higher percent cover, species richness, and diversity.
Direct placement of cover soil resulted in higher percent cover, species richness, and diversity.
stockpiling leads to slower vegetation recovery
direct placement of cover soil supports more rapid succession
Graminoid and bryophyte cover in the stockpiling treatment was 49.5% and 15.3% compared to 12.2% and 12.0% in the direct placement treatment
Graminoid and bryophyte cover in the stockpiling treatment was 49.5% and 15.3% compared to 12.2% and 12.0% in the direct placement treatment
Stockpiling reclaimed sites had less than half of tree cover

edges.original_sentence: In particular, stockpiling reduces the availability and vigor of vegetative propagules and seed, and can lead to increases in the abundance of some weedy species.
